message:
fix: enforce WebSocket idle read deadline to prevent DoS (#3162)

* chore: remove duplicate-detection workflow

The duplicate-detection.yml workflow fired on every new issue and used a
simple 40% keyword-overlap heuristic to label issues as possible-duplicate.
In practice it produced noisy false positives — related but distinct issues
(e.g. #3115 and #3116, two separate NamespaceOverview bugs) were flagged as
duplicates of each other, cluttering the issue list and misleading triage.

Real duplicate detection is handled more accurately during manual triage and
by the auto-triage workflow's AI-assisted analysis.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: improve AI Mission failure UX — actionable errors and Retry button (#3152, #3153, #3154)

- Replace outdated "Configure API Keys" link in no_agent/agent_unavailable errors
  with actionable guidance explaining session interruption and steps to reconnect
- Remove stale API key link from rate-limit error messages
- Add a one-click "Retry Mission" button in the failed mission state that
  re-sends the original user prompt without requiring re-entry

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: return empty lists instead of 400 for unparameterized API calls, mark initializing cluster health (#3143, #3144)

- /api/mcp/clusters: add HealthUnknown=true to clusters with no cached health
  data yet (e.g. immediately after backend boot), preventing false Healthy=false
  flash in the frontend before the first background health refresh completes
- /api/mcp/custom-resources: return empty items list when group/version/resource
  params are absent instead of HTTP 400, to avoid glitchy crash states during
  early React mount phases before data context has hydrated
- /api/gitops/helm-history: return empty history list when release param is
  absent instead of HTTP 400, for the same reason

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: enforce WebSocket idle read deadline to prevent DoS via infinite idle connections (#3150)

Previously, SetReadDeadline was cleared (set to zero) after successful auth,
allowing authenticated connections to remain idle forever without any timeout.
A malicious actor or leaking browser tab could accumulate thousands of idle
sockets and exhaust the Go server's OS file descriptors.

- Add wsIdleTimeout constant (90s)
- Set read deadline to wsIdleTimeout immediately after auth success
- Reset read deadline on every received message so active connections are
  never falsely dropped
- Clients are expected to send periodic ping frames; connections that go
  silent for 90s are dropped automatically

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: andy anderson <andy@andys-MacBook-Pro.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
